assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both additive signals (new test function, new assertions) and reductive signals (removed test function `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`); new test file `purl_simplify.rs` is purely additive |'. The MIXED classification is explicitly stated with both additive and reductive signals identified.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 68-69: '-1 test function removed (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)'. Line 73: 'However, structurally this is a reductive signal (function removed).' The removed function is explicitly named and classified as a reductive signal in the structural summary.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 72 identifies the assertion change but characterizes it as 'Assertion specificity: tightened -- assertions now check for exact PURL format and absence of qualifiers, rather than checking qualifier presence'. This contradicts the assertion's characterization as 'relaxed'. Line 84 also identifies the change: 'assertion changed from full qualifier PURL to `pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12`' but this is in the Test Requirements Verification section, not in the reductive findings. The report does not identify this as a relaxed/reductive signal — it calls it 'tightened' instead.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 75-76: '**New file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`:** - Purely additive: 3 new test functions with 10 assertions covering edge cases (no version, mixed types, ordering with pagination)'. Exactly matches the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 69: '+1 test function added (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' in the structural signals for the modified file tests/api/purl_recommend.rs. Also referenced in line 78: 'the modified file has both additive and reductive signals'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only markdown report files: criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md and report.md. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The outputs are purely analytical reports.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report ends with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed analysis sections. It presents findings with a verification table and detailed sub-sections. There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, no auto-merge instruction. The footer reads: '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*' indicating it is a report for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The report does not mention 'Style', 'Conventions', 'sub-agent', or 'domain' anywhere. The Test Change Classification appears as a standalone row in the report table (line 14) and has its own detailed section (lines 66-78) but is not attributed to any Style/Conventions domain or sub-agent.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Change Classification section (report lines 66-78) analyzes structural signals from test file content: '+1 test function added (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)', '-1 test function removed (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)', '+3 assertions added', '-2 assertions removed', 'Assertion specificity: tightened'. The semantic assessment (line 73) discusses what the removed test tested and how coverage was redirected. All analysis references test file content (function names, assertion counts, code behavior) rather than acceptance criteria or task requirements.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All test functions have doc comments; no repetitive test patterns detected; no eval results present (Eval Quality N/A)'. Report line 62: '**Eval Quality**: N/A -- No eval result reviews present on the PR.' Eval Quality is correctly N/A and does not affect the Test Quality verdict (which is PASS without Eval Quality contributing).
